title: fix: repair window size in macOS
body:
When a program is running on macOS with an external monitor connected, the values obtained through 
`glfw.get_framebuffer_size(self.win)`
will be larger. 
It is more appropriate to use 
`glfw.get_window_size(self.win)`
to retrieve the logical size.

step_plan.summary: Change WindowView.get_framebuffer_size to return the logical window size (glfw.get_window_size) instead of the framebuffer pixel size (glfw.get_framebuffer_size) so macOS with external monitors reports the intended logical dimensions.

steps.operation: Replace call to get_framebuffer_size with get_window_size
steps.reason: On macOS with external HiDPI/Retina monitors, glfw.get_framebuffer_size returns pixel dimensions larger than logical window size; callers of View.get_framebuffer_size in the codebase (viewport setup, projection matrix calculation, background blitting) expect logical window coordinates for layout. Using glfw.get_window_size(self.win) returns the logical window size (in screen coordinates/points) as requested by the PR and centralizes this behavior for all codepaths that call this method.
steps.side_effects: Changes the size reported by WindowView.get_framebuffer_size across the codebase, which will affect viewport/projection setup and any rendering code that consumes this value. Potential visual differences may occur because GL.glViewport expects pixel sizes; if the code previously used the framebuffer pixel size to set the viewport, using logical size could make rendered content scale incorrectly on HiDPI displays. Tests and examples that assert pixel-perfect dimensions or previously relied on framebuffer size should be reviewed and possibly updated. MesaView (offscreen) remains unchanged. No public API signature changes, but dependent modules that assume pixel-accurate viewport must be validated. Recommend running rendering tests on macOS with and without external monitors and adding a note or follow-up PR if platform-aware handling (separate pixel vs logical sizes) is required.
